channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Brown snow falls over Maine town; officials warn against touching, eating it
item.description: Residents of a Maine town are being asked to avoid touching or ingesting the “brown” show that fell in the area.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ: No undercover FBI agents were present for Jan. 6, countering conspiracy theories
item.description: The conclusion rejects theories that undercover FBI agents provoked the attack on the Capitol, which has since prompted charges for more than 1,500 figures involved in the riot.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's employee who called 911 in CEO's shooting is eligible for a reward, but it will take time
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than 400 tips were called into the New York Police Department's Crime Stoppers tip line during the five-day search for a masked gunman who ambushed and fatally shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last week.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WarnerBros. Discovery announces major corporate restructure
item.description: Media conglomerate WarnerBros. Discovery (WBD) announced a major restructuring of its business on Thursday, splitting its linear and streaming arms into two separate companies as it eyes potential mergers and acquisitions in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco names street for Associated Press photographer who captured the iconic Iwo Jima photo
item.description: A block in downtown San Francisco has been renamed for acclaimed photojournalist Joe Rosenthal, who won the Pulitzer Prize for his iconic photo of U.S. Marines raising the flag on the Japanese island of Iwo Jima during WWII.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Build-to-rent homes popping up at faster pace as buyers are priced out
item.description: Companies are maximizing on a tough housing market by building neighborhoods of single-family homes to rent rather than to sell.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declares mistrial in Jimmie 'Jay' Lee murder trial after jury deadlocks
item.description: OXFORD, Miss. — Just before 8:30 p.m. Wednesday, a judge declared a mistrial in the murder trial for University of Mississippi student Jimmie "Jay" Lee. The jury said it could not reach a verdict after more than nine hours. The judge said a new venue may be needed for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Used this parking app? You may qualify for part of $32.8 million settlement
item.description: An estimated 21 million users of the ParkMobile parking app are said to have been impacted by the data breach.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: FAA administrator to resign on Trump swearing-in
item.description: Mike Whitaker is leaving at the start of President-elect Trump’s term, a source familiar with his plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: FBI Director announces his resignation
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced he will resign before President-elect Donald Trump takes office next year and before the end of his 10-year-term.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Mysterious drone flights over New Jersey: Homeland Security shares new details
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi, missing Hawaii woman whose disappearance prompted a massive search, is found safe
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, the missing Hawaii woman whose disappearance prompted a massive search and a missing persons investigation in Los Angeles, has been found safe, police said Wednesday. Kobayashi vanished last month in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Inflation ticks higher as Fed eyes another rate cut
item.description: Inflation picked up slightly in November, according to data released Wednesday by the Labor Department, but likely not by enough to derail the Federal Reserve's plans for another rate cut.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he would do no such thing, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, KTLA 5

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, WGN 9
